channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems — 98 replies — 392.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
SWIFT confirms Chainlink as the bedrock of their systems

Open thread • Replies: 98 • Updated: 2026-06-13 19:36 UTC

OP

Kek fudniggers

Early replies (first 3)

62354945
>Inb4 source
https://www.icmagroup.org/assets/documents/Regulatory/Repo/ICMA-report-DLT-and-repo-part-I-June-2026-040626.pdf

62354971
it's all so tiring
i have sold much more of my stack than i wanted assuming that price would react by now

62354976
>>62354943
Cool, when will I be rich?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62360230
>>62360137
we don't need your prayers stinkbag
we're doing just fine in the material realm without the assistance of your supposed "son of God" who was born in "immaculate conception" (lmao cope for Mary being a whore)

62360247
>>62355053
Three more years.... then my real life can begin

62360331
>>62360230
Im praying still

62360405
>>62360331
well stop

62360577
>>62355053
Yjk this nigger typed 2 more years but decided to throw an extra year in there because it would be too on the nose. Evil fucking nigger kek

62360584
>>62355583
>Flat feet bug gf poster is back
>Link down another -30% since
Sorry bro you better get used to the flapjack feets

62360663
>>62354976
did you take the vax?

62360802
>>62360066
What if link is actually worth something but you can't use it to buy bread. Like kinda like if you had an entire continent in your name but you still need the knowledge to extract resources, work with local governing bodies etc etc

Basically what if we have tokens but are unable to use them because we're goyim

62360825
>>62360137
>duuuuuude it's all a consssssssthhhhpirathy!!!

there he is everyone, our very own sleuth

excuse me, DETECTIVE sleuth

nothing, and i mean NOTHING, gets by this guy. he can sniff out colluding posts in seconds. this guy is GOOD, and i feel ashamed at ever trying to get one by him

afterall, everyone knows that there's nothing more important than guarding the reputation of a dead altcoin on a dead forum


well, you know what we say here...

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62360858
>>62360802
Well, then we're fucked either way.

62360859
>>62360825
>>62360405
Jesus loves you

62360865
>>62360859
religion is the opiate of the masses
or in this case, the stink marines

62360873
>>62360865
That doesn’t change that fact that I don’t hate you and I forgive you.

62360876
>>62360873
your "forgiveness" is rejected, stinkie
how does that make you feel

62361417
>>62360876
feels to me like you're gonna take my whole cock in your ass, and you're gonna like it.

62361431
>>62354976
2030

This year was always the plan for chainlink

62361469
>>62360876
Lilla My, why do you have so much hatred for stinkies?

62361506
I thought green man sergay was going to make me rich...

62361627
>>62358895
COPE

62361699
>>62354943
7 years
7 dollars

62361781
>>62361773

62362456
it's very easy, either it works or I'll keep my stack as a lesson in humility

there is no scenario where I sell

62362461
>>62362456
cool beta attitude, stinker
just admit you lost the game of el lifio

62363300
>>62359501
>>62360137
>>62360331
Jesus is the way truth and life but link is equivalent to the money changer parasites he flipped the tables on who ultimately lose. Invest accordingly.

62363331
>>62363300
truth
jesus would rebuke all linkies
"i do not know you, stinkie"

62363473
>>62361781
CL twitter has been spamming about this shit for years now, nothing is hidden, yet the token price is still garbage

62363725
No matter who wins, the butthurt will be everlasting.
The entertainment is guaranteed

62363750
>>62363473
its effectively hidden from retail because the only thing retail understands is blatant advertising and price. link doesn't get shilled to normalfags the way shit like xrp does (ive never met a single person irl who has heard of chainlink), and nobody cares because the price has been stagnant for 5 years. its actually the perfect setup for a takeoff, though obv even me saying that will make you respond 'bearish'. cold hard truth is this is what disbelief looks like, you are demoralized and nothing will change that but radical price action.

62363975
>>62360876
https://youtu.be/kBYHwH1Vb-c?is=n6RfFklurQgYscVC

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 90 replies — 360.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 90 • Updated: 2026-06-13 19:27 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354799
>>62342905
Elliot only drove a BMW because his dad had some cushy Hollywood job working on films like Hunger Games, etc. Pretty sure he wrote something in his manifesto seething about seeing some guy driving a shitbox and still having a beautiful girl in the passenger seat too.

62354802
>>62342834
ha, i was thinking this read exactly like that post

62354821
>>62342828
When they're writing long form fiction they get a pass.

62355262
>>62342700
Electrical contractor here. I live closer to the beach than you do. It’s kind of funny, most skilled tradesmen look down on people like yourself. We feel sorry for you because in the grand scheme of things - you’re worthless. Unable to build or fix anything. Totally dependent on others. Kinda pathetic

62355284
>>62355262
The mind of a blue collar is truly pathetic.

62355301
>>62342770
I haven’t worked in 1,5 months and i can already feel myself wanting to just become passive and not really being impressed or motivated by anything (brain getting fried by dopamine and serotonine). You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.

62355307
>>62355301
You’re pathetic. Since a little kid, all the classmates of my have been retired.

62355313
>>62355301
> You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
You’re probably just poor. People making a top 10% income from investments tend not to work.

62355322
>>62355301
>1.5 months
You don’t even have a non working lifestyle built yet.

62355326
>>62355307
This may be the funniest claim I've seen you make. Absolutely nobody from your school does any kind of work at all? Most people do find things to keep themselves occupied with, beyond mere shitposting on an anonymous uzbekistani finger painting notification board.

62355338
>>62355326
Maybe we grew up in different classes.
On one hand, /biz/ loves to larp as rich and successful and on the other hand, loves to larp as muh productive and I don’t like doing nothing.
Fucking jokes.
I hear people say what you say all the time. Yet look at retired people, I don’t see you saying shit to them. Yet when it is someone young and not working, suddenly they are “unproductive.” Even though these people are clearly rich.

62355342
>>62355307
>>62355313
>>62355322
Kek did i trigger you too much?

62355360
>>62355342
I have never in my life looked at someone not working and thought anything bad.
Growing up, my dad retired when I was like 5. By 10, the parents of all my school friends were retired long ago.
By past college, the differences between the haves and have nots became noticeable because college is where you truly see class differences because high school and under tend to mainly kids from your neighborhood.
You cannot imagine life without work yet kids that grew up like me were surrounded by adults that don’t work. It is the normal. People like me just carry on the lifestyle.
People from successful families don’t break their back.

62355375
>>62355301
Depends what you mean by work. But any occupation today is not a good use of time. Like work as in what? Something you need to get a college degree for? White collar?
Or something blue collar? That’s even dumber.
Starting your own business? Okay sure, if you want to start it as a hobby..?
I don’t know. People build lifestyles when they don’t need to work to live.
Some travel, some have many social gatherings
>Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
Oh yeah, the bottom 90% definitely feel alive struggling lol.
Struggling for what? Lmao.

62355404
>>62355301
Sometimes I see the kids in my neighborhood. This isnt an ultra rich 100m a year neighborhood or millions a month neighborhood but in 10-20 years when these kids grow up, it would be a waste of time for them to get a “job.”
Get it?

62357658
>>62355301
Rabbis rabbiposting as usual. Nice try but no, i won't work for (((you)))

62359024
>>62342770
I have 300k...where should i buy a house and get on gibbs? Caliwali?

62359462
>>62342700
I mean someone has to like do stuff u need someone to actually do stuff

62359491
>>62342770
This is called capitalism btw. You either exploit others or u work until you die. Hello knock knock HELLO this is basic capitalism. Wake up everything u guys complain about is due to market forces the Jews are a fantasy of cohesion to keep your little world simple bigga

62360989
Never worked a day in my life and I would never shit on a trade they keep my water running and the electricity flowing. Would you trust a 3rd worlder to wire up your outlet in your house and not expect to die in your sleep when that shit catches fire fuck no I'm going to pay a white American 150$ an hour to do that shit right. Without their goods and services our money doesn't mean shit and I might aswell get raped by a pack of niggers.

62361159
All work is automateable. No one of you should feel safe.

62361494
>>62344611
>the producing members
The secret is these types wouldnt know what to do in life other than work. Their life centers around it. You're supposed to exploit this in them

62363064
>>62361159
What about work on new products that have never been fully before
Someone has to build it first for a robot to even try

62363066
>>62360989
Based

62363074
>>62342905
BMW stock is worth about $25 kek

62363190
>>62361494
Yeah.
Half of Americans own less than 200k in networth but these people try to call me a loser cause I don’t need work.

62363514
>>62342700
The funniest part of this larp spam where you autistically have back and forth conversations about class and wealth with normies is that even in your fantasies you hold a captive audience of blue collar workers and poor office wagies, meaning that whatever your position in life, you're really not that far above them.

62363942
>>62363514
Shut the fuck up loser.
I am way above them.
They are called the average. Irl, most people are office workers at best.
Then you have business owners (this doesn’t automatically mean they’re rich) and you have those in higher tier salaries.
Online, there are more ruralfags and suburban people and/or blue collar.
That’s literally what it is like.

62363944
>>62363514
Watch your fucking mouth.

62363954
>>62363514
I am so far above them mon frere.
I live in a big city and there are tons of rich people here so I was uh huh, average salary here definitely 150k to 200k.
Nope, it is $65k.
Do you know how fucking poor everyone is you pathetic worm scum.

item.title: So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. — 68 replies — 128.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo.

Open thread • Replies: 68 • Updated: 2026-06-13 19:07 UTC

OP

So far this year, I've lost 23k betting on Nintendo. How the fuck is it legal for them to destroy so much shareholder value? I WANT MY FUCKING MONEY BACK NOW!!

Early replies (first 3)

62348099
>>62348037
You did not lose anything, shoe shine boy. change your diapers and calm the FUCK down. Because you didn't sell yet. Right?

62348149
Better price to buy m8. You don't believe in the business anymore or somethin

62348170
If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead, I don't know what to tell you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62357443
>>62348037
Nintendo is sitting on an assload of cash. they don't care about shareholders.

62360010
>>62348170
Nintendo is going to continue doing well, but it was overpriced before.
Videogames are not a dying industry, they are just no longer a growth industry. The reason gaming used to seem like an amazing investment oportunity until recently was that there were way more people going into the hobby than going out and the first big generations of gamers were hitting their mid/late 30s when earnings start peaking. It didn't matter whether game companies were good or not because the gaming pie was growing so fast that their slice would grow too.
Now that gaming is as normiefied as it gets and there are adult gamers of all ages, the pie is not growing anymore and companies have to actually be good businesses if they want to survive and grow. That's why companies that were already known for a decade to be shit like Ubisoft seem to be imploding all of a sudden.

62360025
>>62349964
Now look at the 5 year returns for Advantest and Tokyo Electron and say that again.

62360035
>>62350352
>>all good games are made by indie developers
>why would you bet on any of the big companies? they are creatively bankrupt bloated bureaucracies
Even if most good games are made by smaller developers nowadays, most actual money printing game franchises that actually move the needle when it comes to investing are still big corporate IPs.

62360245
>>62360010
This is one of the reasons why I am hesitant to invest in gaming. Feels like I would be ripping off young first time investors who pump those stocks when a popular game comes out. Like OP. Anything industrial it is often boomers who died of old age bag holding before their relatives could retrieve their stock portfolios at the right time. Or they do retrieve a whale's when it is crabbing and there is a dip to be had. I get less guilt on my conscious screwing over a Boomer than a Zoomer.

62360284
>>62360245
Zoomers are getting cocky and obnoxious, acting like concepts they come up with haven't ever been heard of and tried before. I await the day for the alphas to grow up and shit all over them.

62360459
>>62360284
Still better than Boomers and gen X ever were. And that is saying something. I have more in common with Zoomer 20 years younger than me than a lead maxed gen X who is 10 years older. Very refreshing to have lots more people to talk to without the lead brain damage. Haven't heard as many good ideas circulating widely until they grew up.

Seriously Gen X were scum who often didn't even give a shit their own kids needed to be potty trained until a kindergarten teacher told them they needed to be. Most of whom were totally normal kids of average intelligence they neglected that badly when it came to teaching them everyday things. This is who were most of the Zoomers parents. They could have turned out better if it weren't for that. Every other generation was horrified by 4 year olds still in diapers. They really didn't care like a drugged out hippy until the kid was old enough to go to public school.

62360515
>>62348037
No one guys Nintenslop anymore. Millennials are the last generation that cares about it, but are ageing out of gaming now.

62360525
You shouldnt buy a stock unless you plan on keeping it for 5 to 10 years

62360531
>>62360459
That's cool and all, but those fags will die off soon, and then they'll just end up shifting blame to kikes or anyone else besides themselves. Not to mention this faggy generation is just as tech illiterate and censorious as the boomers they bitch about.

62360534
0 mainline heavy hitters yet. Metroid was in dev hell for a decade.

62360549
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son.
Such as? The steam deck is now overpriced and other handheld PCs are even more expensive for similar offerings. So you're buying your son a system that's at least 2-3 more expensive than Nintendo's gaming system to play kid games that are available on every platform and even phones.... kek what a tard

62360629
>>62348278
>They are getting cooked by real handhelds now and I’m going to be buying another handheld isn’t a switch for my son
Making money out of handheld consoles depends on just 2 factors: How cheap can you make them and how much money can you get from software sales on that console.
Nintendo are the only handheld manufacturer with enough sales volume to get the best manufacturing prices. They are able to even commision custom silicon like Apple does with their phones to optimize things further, something that is completely out of reach of smaller players.
Nintendo also gets a ton of money from game sales and subscriptions as they limit things like the switch internet unless you pay and own game franchises that only release on their hardware.
Meanwhile their competitors need to spend more money to make an equivalent product and the only one that actually get revenue from software sales is Valve.
There's always going to be more powerful handhelds than whatever Nintendo makes as they don't design their stuff for performance, but there's not going to be a single handheld product that is as effective at generating net income as Nintendo's until someone starts getting closer to their sales volume.
Whenever Nintendo doesn't offer the best performance per dollar ratio, it is because they are selling their hardware at a premium due to how strong their brand is, not because they are incapable of making money at a lower pricepoint. Their competitors meanwhile are always running razor thin margins and any major impact to manufacturing costs needs an immediate readjustment to the sales price, as exemplified by the huge price bump the Deck got this month.

TL;DR: Nintendo doesn't make shit deals out of their products because they are incapable of offering something better, but because they have such an advantageous position that they don't need to. If someone ever gets close enough to force them to compete on prices/performance more, they will.

62360701
>>62360531
Millennials who hold good jobs are the most censorious. They were screened by Jewmers for that role. The rest found out recently that supporting that dick sucking networking will not give them high paying jobs if they didn't already have them. That door was already closed on them. With the last seats given out to the worst best friends of nepo baby Millennials they could find.

62360704
There was no normie hype for Switch 2. It was just more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361034
There was no normie hype for the iphone 6, or 7, or whatever number. It was more of the same. The writing was on the wall.

62361130
>picking stocks
Why do you retards keep doing this? You can't even have sex and by extension lack any social skills. You don't network. You don't get insider info. All the info and "intelligence" you get about companies and their future performance comes from mainstream sources like Bloomberg and Yahoo finance. You aren't built for trading or even decided where to invest.
Put your arrogance aside for a minute and start buying index funds or your destiny will be complete destitution and bitterness.

You have been warned.

62361211
>>62348170
>If you couldn't tell the vidya industry is dead
I don’t know the statistics behind it, but it sure does feel that way. Same with the movie industry. Nothing is like what it used to be.

62361368
>>62361211
Both the gaming and movie industry are dead. The level of creativity is long gone. Most of the best games that have ever existed are all games that came out early-mid 2000s and before. Most of the gaming that I do today is on the emulator. 8 and 16 bit games are timeless and, unlike 32/64 bit stuff, the graphics age well. It’s also quick to pick up and play, and they are simply fun. Same goes with arcade games. There’s some decent stuff on steam but a lot of creators simply rely on good graphics to carry their games. Besides, there’s only so many ideas that you can come up with for games before it gets old. All the genres have been rehashing the same type of games and all they’ve done is tweak the graphics and stories a bit. It’s unfortunate that newfags will never get to experience how fun the gaming atmosphere of the past used to be like.

62361513
>>62361130
I have made more money doing the opposite of what they tell me half the time. It just takes a bullshit detector skills to figure out if they are lying or not and looking at other company data they are ignoring. Sometimes I have noticed that Blackrock invests like low information investors and then tells normies to pump their bags. In fact just look to see how much mutual funds are already invested into a stock just ready to rug pull. At better strategy is to invest in other things and wait for them to bite. Then you rug pull them. Seriously I won big on investing in mining this way. Nearly everything I invested in they pumped once enough people familiar with the industry took notice. The scary thing is I am a collage dropout who never studied finance. Just self taught in my 30's and a little trial and error. Silver spoon nepo babies can just invest in a little of everything in a sector, hope for the best in a market cycle, and still make money.

62361516
Video gaming has collapsed and recovered multiple times but zoom zooms are too young to remember.

62361524
>>62361130
90% of my money is in boomer mutual funds and I take the remaining 10% and pick stocks. It isn't always one or the other.

62361639
>>62354555
Yeah I don't get people, when I become involved with a company I turn into something of a cheerleader. It's just fun cheering on SK even if I have never heard of them before.

62361715
People can barely afford groceries and you're gambling on bing bing bow companies? Read the room ffs..

62362175
>>62348037
Would have probably made more money on pokemon cards. Anyway dumb as fuck to bet on a company that only knows how remake games about some short fat italian plumber.

62363751
>>62348037
Did you sell?
You didn't lose anything yet if you are holding.
Nintendo will be fine in long term.
Eventually they have enough blockbuster titles and Switch 2 will sell like crazy.

62363794
Time to buy

62363812
>>62360701
>my two moms
Americans think that they aren't still fighting for this just because their noses aren't being rubbed in it anymore. Crazy.

62363883
>>62348037
even when the games industry is performing well (it definitely is not rn) it still sucks compared to other industries. i probably know games better than other industries but i still wouldn't touch it with a 10ft pole

62363911
>>62363812
The underlings supporting this for free are mostly Boomers at this point. No Kings rally was one giant Boomer rally. With an occasional Gen X and elder Millennial. Just about everyone else was obviously dragged there by older people. I would rather get a slow boomer stock where a boomer acked at the wrong time being the bagholder. And in this case it is mostly a matter of circumstance. Young normies aren't as insufferable anymore. These people have to be paid to do this in large numbers. But I would not mind knowing an infantile Millennial normie will be bag holding. Who were the last dick suckers to be rewarded generously. It is just that I would also hurt some zoomers in the process.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JYbgfgE96VI&list=FLkFEa2pg_tj954pX0TsPtzw&index=3
The youngest sort of people I don't mind bag holding. If they support this sort of crap that is. Not wishing death but they deserve to have less money from propping up a system that fucks me over.

item.title: The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. — 45 replies — 108.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants.

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-06-13 19:14 UTC

OP

The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. He's upper class. And has 3 passports and owns properties and businesses (wealth) around the world. He isn't waiting on a paycheck to drive down to Costco.
Hint: it's a Rothschild property.
Making even a million bucks a year writing code for Elon or Bezos or Brin does NOT put you in the upper class. At all.
I am European and only we have class. We invented class.

Early replies (first 3)

62356062
>>62356048
Before AI, you could write code for ten years and then buy this.

62356086
Got so mad in the other thread you thought you would start a new thread about it LMAO

62356191
this is that same polish guy who was seething in the other thread about class or whatever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358945
>>62358433
I need to pay the bills
>>62358695
Based. You are not far from the Kingdom of God, but don't kid yourself, there's still a lot to do

62358989
>>62358606
>"Death is the great equalize...-ACCKKK!!!"

62359005
>>62358989

62359167
>>62356048
>dickriding gloating by association
>this lifestyle was real in my mind
Cool story, op
Lunch is over, time to clock back in

62359177
>>62358695
Based

62359194
>>62358695
>Jesus Christ
>demonic kike

62359210
>>62358989
It still is, but you can live your life like a beta peasant trying to extend your life seeking a philosopher stone (if only to perpetuate your suffering) or live like a chad pharaoh accepting death and building his own sarcophagus and tomb during his lifetime. Two different games, yet the same ending

62359212
>>62358695
Where the fuck is Christ? Why has he foresaken me? Is he even fucking real?

62359254
>>62358989
You don't even know the name of the guy who built those.

62359275
>>62356048
yeah and he is never happy and is made of the same carbon we all are. class is fake. wealth is fake. you can be happy with nothing easier than being happy with everything.

62359283
>>62359212
the real god made himself known to man a lot longer than 2000 years ago. it was pre history, so of course we know nothing about it. abrahamic tradition is nonsense.

62359313
>>62356048
Damn I triggered this barely literate 2nd generation immigrant to America (note I never called him an American - he isn't) so bad. Damn, I am almost sorry since it's Friday but not sorry since you're brown.

62359371
>>62356048
I'm sure his life sucks everyone has a reason to cry about shit no amount of stucco will save your body from pain suffering and misery it is inescapable

62359452
>>62358695
God bless you please pray for me.

62359520
>class is following as many rules as possible while frowning and spending lots of money
Wow sounds like a good time sign me up

62359522
>>62358695
Oh please spare me the weak cope philosophy where Jesus christ literally gets abandoned by God when he needs him the most on the cross so there is no guarantee. God is a child's fantasy of cohesion that the world is simple and suffering has meaning, you think someone is watching you at all times and ur not smart enough to realize that emptiness is structural and is born out of language and it's a insurmountable gap that you can only move through and not ever reach. When u move towards it without expecting anything..that is when u can finally act finally be free from god at least,.to relate to the symbolic world of language differently in a way where u can actually do what you want.

62359740
>>62359522
You’re a fag or a drug or porn addict. Maybe all three. Fuck off retard.

62360058
>>62359212
>>62359283
"The fool says in his heart that there is no God"

Maybe try turning away your sinful ways, repenting and searching scripture instead of looking for handouts. Or you could just live out your days as a hard hearted rancid transgressor and go to the grave an unbelieving fool. Nothing anyone says can change your blackened heart. The Holy Spirit does that work.

62360102
>>62360058
why is a 24 year old mulatto born again christian zoomer trying to give me life advice?

62360116
>>62359522
you have free will to do what you want. But be warned that you lash out at the unblemished Lamb of God. The Alpha and the Omega who healed every infirmity and cast out demons from the land. The Good Shepherd who has defeated and and is Seated at the right hand of Power. The sad thing about people like you is that you are so fallen and your nature is so corrupt that you cannot even hear the Truth if it was read to you. Even the demons themselves that Jesus drove out of people KNEW that he was the Son of the Most High. Repent brother. The Kingdom of Heaven is at hand. We know not the day nor the hour of His return

62360814
>>62359005
>these death monuments will be renouned and respected and not some dinky little tourist hotsp-ACK

62361163
>>62357608
Why do people hate on these posts instead of try to figure out ways to improve their quality of life to have similar?

That's the whole point of this board. Instead of hating on people showing off their wealth figure out how to get it too.

I thought it was only like this on hip hop forums. Everyone hat s on each other and then refuse all ways of getting to it.

62361183
>>62359210
Or stop all or nothing thinking and find a way to live well so you can have wealth and not have to compromise yourself in any way?

62362527
>>62356048
Why would I want such a monstrosity.

62362541
>>62360116
>unfathomably based life speaking minister of /biz
I will miss you when the tranny kike mods ban you for speaking truth, but I know you will bear your cross with dignity.

62362654
>>62356048
why would you even want this? what are you going to use it for? throw parties 24/7 for people you fucking hate? If you live their 24/7 the nice view and garden just become mundane like everything else only now you have a lot less money

62362675
>>62362527
You’re not European, you would not get it.

62362885
>>62358695
>stay poor and let the Jews control wealth and power like a good goy

62363916
>>62356048
everyone there is a pedophile and you'd be asked to leave if you said nigger or faggot. pass.

62363922
>>62356062
Is becoming an expert in AI still a way to make money at all?

item.title: Day 1743 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M — 10 replies — 40.0/hr

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-13 19:28 UTC

OP

Day 1743 of snailposting every day until BTC is at 1M

Early replies (first 3)

62363268
kys

62363373
accidentally

62363383
>>62363373
dont do this :(

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363388
>>62363383
>do this

OK, only accidentally!

62363404
>>62363388
it's very cruel, the foaming you see is its desperate attempt to safe itself with mucus production. just pick up the snail and put it elsewhere, they're not pests.

62363898
>>62363404
Lol

62363907
>>62363898
psychopaths should be organ harvested

62363912
>>62363907
I've only become a psychopath because I have to gamble with shitcoins just to survive.

62363929

62363957
>>62363912

item.title: how the FUCK can I invest in semiconductors and spacex from Moscow? — 21 replies — 18.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
how the FUCK can I invest in semiconductors and spacex from Moscow?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-13 18:31 UTC

OP

how the FUCK can I invest in semiconductors and spacex from Moscow? This pozzed country doesnt let me invest in anything not even the russian stock market

Early replies (first 3)

62361955
move to thailand

62361960
>>62361945
>expecting wholesome gif
>it's someone dying
This is why you subhuman faggot what is wrong with you people?? When will you become civilised? Then you can join the rest of humanity at the investment table.

62361965
>>62361960
he was fine though just a little disoriented dont be a tranny

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361977
>>62361945
Invest in it by overthrowing Putin for being a race traitor. Russia is importing 800,000 jeets annually. That's worse than the UK.

62361990
>>62361945
Don't you have domestic scams?

62361996
>>62361945
You can buy exposure to most of the big semi stocks + spcx on hyperliquid. Non-KYC (for now) so just get a vpn and some usdc and go for it, if that's what you want to do. It's a perps exchange so you don't really own the stock, on the other hand it's a perps exchange so you can leverage your 50 rubles into 250 rubles of exposure if you want to.

62362003
>>62361996
thanks for the tip

62362010
>>62361945
In Soviet Russia, company owns you!

62362035
>>62361945
an hero and hope that you come back as an American in the next life

62362336
>>62361945
move to Zelenograd

62362422
Cock blocking nanny state

62362531
Can russians not even use ibkr?

62362698
>>62361960
Russians and Eastern Europeans are living the most depressed lives
Dying is considered a reprieve in those countries

62362738
>>62361945
Tell Putin he sucks ass, escape Puccia, do whatever you want with your life.

Or go die in a trench in Ukraine, your choice.

62362752
Its another indian meme thread, There is no reason why he wouldn't be able to invest in the Russian stock market, or in local gold and bond products (unless he is actually a muslim migrant with no citizenship). Moreover there are international stock brokers that do accept Russians. Finally for Russian it might be a better idea to invest in Cambricon and SMIC, than to put money in jewish stock markets.

62362754
>>62361945
I don't know maybe start caring and discussing about politics instead treating it as something you have absolutely no influence on like a fucking weather.

62362757
>>62362698
im bulgarian and i live better than you

62362976
>>62362757
>lel the table-let thinks he's living better than me
I have a full-sized table and one of those rugs too.

62363008
>>62361945
Wake up you moron.
All countries suck. Some suck a tad less. You're white. Why are you stuck in a shithole? Try to go somewhere else before slimes and poos ruin it. You may be able to invest too. But don't buy SpaceX until it dips well under 100, only dolts buy high.

62363714
>>62363008
Based

62363819
>>62362757
that seems like the average shit house in bulgaria. I don't know what you're bragging about. You larped being rich in another thread

item.title: Who was the worst CEO/executive in business history? — 16 replies — 18.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Who was the worst CEO/executive in business history?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-06-13 18:45 UTC

OP

Who was the worst CEO/executive in business history? Not counting outright criminals like SBF.

Early replies (first 3)

62362011
>>62361985
In what time frame? In certain ways Jack Welch is an excellent CEO and in others he is one of the worst.

62362026
>>62361985
Richard Fuld

62362048
>>62361985
There have been a lot of dumb guys. First to come to mind is Apple's first CEO, Michael Scott. He was basically just some guy who worked in a chip factory so they made him the CEO for that tiny bit of experience. After a little bit of success, he got stressed out and had a mental breakdown. He fired most of the company while laughing. Then afterwards he got really drunk in the office to celebrate everyone who still had a job.

As of right now, Ubisoft's CEO, Yves Guillemot, is a total moron. The gaming industry has changed a lot, yet he refuses to innovate. He won't even sell the company unless he gets to remain as CEO, which scared off potential investors. Rather than making new games, his plan is to remake old games. The problem with remaking old games is that his company has essentially been doing that for the past 15 years, which is the entire reason why sales are down in the first place. We are seeing Ubisoft collapse in real time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362055
>>62362048
>He fired most of the company while laughing. Then afterwards he got really drunk in the office to celebrate everyone who still had a job.
The Wework guy did that too.

62362062
>>62361985
Steve Cohen as CEO of the mest

62362073
Charles Hotsctittison, CEO of the failed scam coin (with no DApps) “Cardano”.

62362150
>>62361985
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bTAKSBvuT-A

Legendary interview.

62362218
>>62362150
This guy was a pure retard kek I remember getting the Xbox one as a gift I hated it I couldn’t play a damn offline game without WiFi have to constantly sign in and also the age limit restriction.

62362243
>>62361985
Aside anyone in the current administration and elon musk...? I would say NIssan and Mitsubishi, both companies have Ceo's that rammed their companies through the ground hard.

62362283
>>62362243
?

62362313
Nortel ceo Mike Zafirovski

62362562
>>62361985
Carly fiorina

62362577
>>62361985
Gregg Steinhafel

62362584
Obviously he's not the worst or even close to it but Mark Zuckerberg has made some exceptionally dogshit decisions.

62363824
>>62361985
>Not counting outright criminals like SBF.
All businesses fail on a long enough timeline, and all CEOs engage in fraud to some degree, some just go full retard. Lots of people work their way up and it appears they are competent because their actions/decsions were strictly controlled by a competent senior, but once the get to a kep postion and can do as they please they bankrupt or nearly bankrupt the company. There are many, many examples, espcially since the meme of appointting DEI candidates to CEO positions. One regular retard is Randall Lynn Stephenson who almost destroyed ATT. Look at all the CEOs of the big banks and fiancial companies all with MBAs or PhDs from the top univeristies that destroyed their companies and need infinate money bailouts to save the whole system in 2008/2009. Do not forget George W. Bush graduated with and MBA from Harvard and oversaw the regulation and near destruction of the US economy. When I was taking my accounting classes I had to do a project, I believe it was a presentation on FASB 57, something like that. What I found was for every notable financial collapse of the some major company the separate accounting agency, like Arthur Anderson, was fully aware of the fraud and part of the fraud for excessive fees.

62363858
>>62361985
Greg Lindberg

item.title: MARS MISSION IS A COMPLETE SCAM. IT IS PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO SUSTAIN HUMAN LIFE ON MARS. IT WILL NEVER HAPPEN. IT'S A… — 21 replies — 17.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
MARS MISSION IS A COMPLETE SCAM. IT IS PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO SUSTAIN HUMAN LIFE ON MARS. IT WILL NEVER HAPPEN. IT'S A…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-13 18:28 UTC

OP

MARS MISSION IS A COMPLETE SCAM.

IT IS PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO SUSTAIN HUMAN LIFE ON MARS.

IT WILL NEVER HAPPEN.

IT'S A CASH GRAB.

Early replies (first 3)

62358478
>>62358421
why does he need more cash if not to go to mars?

62358533
>>62358478
To be more rich

62358563
They have enough compute for really good deepfakes. There was never any intention to go. We learned from NASA that all you need is pretty pictures and th spacekeks will froth

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358665
>>62358478
For jews its a dick measuring contest.

62359904
Impossible? No. Difficult? Yes. Very, very difficult.

62360411
>>62358421

62360427
>>62358421
It's not just that the Mast mision is unviable, it is that they are telling you that a company that can't manage to make money on Earth is somehow going to make money on Mars kek.

62360573
>>62358421
More importantly in the context of /biz/, it isn't profitable

62360832
>>62358563
why compute and not computation

62360954
>>62360573
Correct. Not profitable in the lifetime of any living person. But someday maybe.

62361241
It'll happen but not for another 100 years at least, most especially at the snails pace tech has "advanced" since the new millennium.

62361299
>>62361241
Tech has advanced extremely fast, but all that advancement has happened on the digital side since that's where the money is.
"Let the goyim deal in the real world" and all that.

62361307
>>62358478
Fund his many other failing businesses

Mars is red, dry, Antarctica but hey at least it has extremely high radiation levels too, enough to give you cancer in weeks. Oh and long term low gravity effects that would cause god knows what health issues. Oh and the soil is toxic as well.


Mars colonization is a scam

62363330
>>62358421
I read that the atmosphere is so thin that it feels like a warm summer's day at your feet at 20°C but your head would freeze instantly at -150°C

62363343
Mars is stupid, cold and empty.
Lets find a similar Earth in the Andromeda Galaxy.

62363397
>>62363343
what if they have niggers too

62363420
Do people realise Mars' gravity is like 1/3 of Earth? It's closer to the Moon's gravity than to the Earth, you'd have to smash like 2 Mercury's worth of material into Mars to get Earth-like gravity. Anybody on Mars will eventually get fucked up from their bodies being in such a low-g environment not to mention what would happen to soft-boned babies.

62363426
>>62363397
Imagine geniuses building a device to replace space metrics to "travel", find another Earth like planet, it's teeming with life, esrablish a colony, and the smartest animal there is roughly half as smart as the clever mathematicians and astronauts who travelled. So instead of providing cuteness and pats, they wreck the colony and ask for resources because it's our fault they ate everything.

62363459
whites just like to go venture out

62363771
>>62358421
>spend years training astronauts
>billions upon trillions spent
>first humans arrive on mars
>they are drooling retards with mush brains melted from space radiation
money well

62363802
>>62358421
>uhhhmm actually everything is a le scam and ai is just a hecking chatbot!
I see too many midwits parroting this

item.title: I've bought LTC at around $45 back in 2013. — 4 replies — 3.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've bought LTC at around $45 back in 2013.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-13 18:29 UTC

OP

I've bought LTC at around $45 back in 2013. Should I just keeping hodling and at least wait for a break even or maybe sell now at a loss?

Early replies (first 3)

62363232
>>62363106
Its should easily go back over 100$ again but if you're to impatient then sell it.

62363299
BCH functions better than LTC that functions better than BTC

yet the valuations are in reverse.

62363376
>>62363106
Litecoin is a dead project. You're almost breaking even at the moment, you may as well dump it before it collapses even further.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363804
>>62363106
>>62363232
MWEB extension is being implemented. Although i heard there was a hack that was miraculously avoided due to the hacker's mistakes. They could have scammed all the litecoin in the MWEB extension. That would have been catastrophic.
THis was avoided however so if litecoin manages to implement this successfully it could be the cause for a major pump.

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 66 replies — 36.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 66 • Updated: 2026-06-13 17:51 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362583
He's ran a very good public relations campaign the last few decades. He made sure that his name was associated with his products. Tesla is synonymous with Elon Musk

62362589
>>62361452
Guy I'm stuck in e13 figure hell and people are making fun of me online. What do?

62362674
>>62362449
maybe the data center angle is just what they are selling to the institutional investors, and they really do believe in space exploration?

62362684
>>62361478
>robotics
he hasn't sold a single robot

62362688
>>62362068
he received massive subsidies the entire time in tesla
That's not his doing. Politicians wanted EVs no matter if he produced them or someone else did
They were already was creating a car before he joined Tesla

62362689
>>62361452
Of all the famous billionaires, Musk's companies are the only truly innovative ones. Like many of us, I don't like his liberalism or his transhumanism, but it's undeniable that the "hype" that fuels his investments is based on the significant chance that he can pull of what he says. Afterall everyone wrote off starlink and autonomous cars.

I do find it irritating how people can't talk about him or spacex in good faith. They hide behind investment language they don't understand when the truth is that they hate him and his companies because the media told them to. The regime media has had it in for him ever since he bought twitter since they had a near total monopoly on information before that point. You're seeing just how much they hate the situation by the fact that the UK grooming gang discussion in parliament was hijacked by deranged leftists into a 2 minute hate against Musk.
These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example, a truly hideous "person" who has done nothing except harm humanity and steal money.

62362700
>>62361452
Now that he's a trilly, do you goys think this bitch will still settle for that $500k/year he was giving her or will that 10x soon?

62362803
>>62362689
>they hate him and his companies because the media told them to
I simply hate all billionaires because it's fucked

62362809
>>62362689
>The regime media
the ones who pushed EVs?
>These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example
you mean the currency manipulator who "broke the bank of england" ?
who did he steal money from?

62362814
>>62361452
In a sea of grifting the guy offers at least a unique perspective on his multiple scams. For that he is awarded 1 trillion dollars

62362818
>>62361452
ask yourself which space sector company can dislodge SpaceX from government contract welfare. they are now too embedded, much like Microsoft with their shitty products, and will always recieve enough taxpayer gibs to keep them profitable while also paying investors. literally nobody else can compete

62362863
>>62361452
It's a mystery

62362883
>>62361478
He makes promises he can't keep and retards like you fall for it every time which keep the stocks inflated.

62362985
>>62361452
It's paper networth. His net worth is in stock shares. The stock market has nothing to do with real life and is just a popularity contest. Buying a share of Tesla and thinking you own a piece of Tesla despite it never paying a dividend or doing a buyback is no different than buying dodgecoin and thinking u own a stake in the dog. His stocks only value is just what ever u can tell to for to the next person. All he does is pull from science fiction and that keeps his stock popular. It's literally no different from a casino. Ultimately this gambling money comes from the petro dollar

62363104
>>62362557
Poorfag doesn't bussines

62363313
>>62361452
People are so impressed by the self-landing rockets and the sci fi visions that they throw their money at him without any thought on whether the numbers are reasonable.

62363342
>>62362818
US government contracts are only 21% of SpaceX revenue ("Customer A" in the prospectus), and the growth potential is limited.

There is no strong vendor lock-in with SpaceX like there is with Microsoft. The US government absolutely wants to diversify its vendor base and will give contracts to other vendors at earliest opportunity

62363359
>>62361966
she also has an unlimited customer base

62363375
>>62361478
>EVs and robots
requires the us government to ban chinese evs and robots because they're raping him in price lmao, if elon is worth 1 trillion cuz muh robots then unitree is worth 100 trillion lmao

62363382
>>62361478
He's not ahead of the competition in anything except space.

For a time, he was ahead in EVs. This he achieved by leveraging advancements in battery technology by Japanese and Korean companies, while other car companies were hesitant because they did not want to cannibalize their existing ICE product lines.

62363405
>>62361452
everything is fake and gay in this economy and World retard
>SpaceX's valuation is expected to be nearly $1.8 trillion, revenue was 18 billion in 2025
>Toyota valuation is at 230 billion, revenue in 2025 was 320 billion

62363434
>>62363405
we don't need more cars
we need more spaceships
simple as

62363442
>>62361499
I'll believe sodium batteries when i see them. The voltage curve of the ones i've seen so far are horrible--a car at 100% charge might be fast but at 50% charge might be dog slow.

62363501
>>62363442
No no everything that comes out of China is bleeding edge and perfect on the first try.

62363544
>>62361452
because you keep giving him money

62363603
>>62363375
>Trusting chink shit
Not in my lifetime xiao

62363614
>>62361452
>all ''muh equity' meme, actually has nothing
Nearly all of the modern, popular billionaire class isn't even rich. Augustus or Mansa Musa mog.

62363633
>>62363442
They are a real thing. But they are not premium batteries for cars because of their rather low energy density. Much better suited for energy storage where you don't care about space or weigh that much if the specs are otherwise good.

62363679
>>62362689
>Like many of us, I don't like his liberalism or his transhumanism
...what? When people talk about musk being liberal or a transhumanist isn't the first thing that comes to mind

62363689
>>62363679
Maybe it should be

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 94 replies — 25.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:01 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62352286
>>62350315
Ethereum has certainly built one of the strongest ecosystems in crypto.

62354098
>>62351129
You have a point there

62354803
>>62350283
Maybe this is how people finally start exploring BTC in DeFi. Participation is still tiny. Imagine what happens if even 1% starts becoming 2%, then 5%.

62354820
>>62351106
I've actually earned more from memecoins than utility coins lately lol.

62354827
>>62339328
Anyone bullish on avalanche?

62355050
>>62339328
I sold when the Epstein files dropped. Feels good.

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

item.title: Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to… — 29 replies — 15.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to…

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-06-13 17:46 UTC

OP

Remember the classic Wall Street rule: "When the shoeshine boy gives you stock tips, it's time to sell."Fast forward to today, June 12, 2026.SpaceX goes public (ticker: SPCX) as the largest IPO in human history.The absolute pinnacle of "shoeshine boy" indicators unfolds before our eyes.

Look at the massive retail mania.Brokers are literally bending over backward to push high-risk equity onto everyday people.

Fidelity casually drops its early SpaceX asset requirement by 99.996%.Went from a $500,000 balance requirement down to a measly $2,000 just so anyone can buy in.

Everyday normies who have never read a balance sheet are getting allocations.SpaceX targets a massive 20% to 30% retail allocation just to feed the public frenzy.

Early replies (first 3)

62361322
Look at the underlying financial reality.The stock prices in pure unadulterated "Musk premium" while totally ignoring the numbers.

Priced at $135 a share, meaning investors paid 94 times trailing revenue.The company has an accumulated deficit of $41.3 billion.Lost $4.9 billion net last year alone.

The $2+ trillion valuation literally assumes a 75-fold earnings growth by 2035.

Valuation relies on unproven sci-fi assumptions like building AI data centers in outer space.

62361333
>>62361316
they're spending worthless dollars and they know it.

$135 per share? That's how much I make per employee per hour doing nothing. That's the value of an hour's work in dollars in the US

basically worthless dollars.

62361335
peak euphoria.Retail hype pumps the stock up 19% on day one to close at $160.95. Elon becomes the world's first trillionaire

SpaceX immediately starts selling merch reading "The future is public" at the listing. nasdaq rules mean it will be fast-tracked into the Nasdaq-100 in just 15 trading days.

Passive retirement funds and normie index accounts will be forced to buy it automatically.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361347
it's a financial bubble beyond humanity's collective compre-fucking-hension

62361357
>>62361347
That's why it's Space, Space X, the bubble will burst being Earth

62361361
>>62361357
beyond*

62361365
>>62361316
We need exit liquidity you obnoxious poorshit.

62361385
>>62361316
The shoeshine boy has been telling me that capitalism is evil and SpaceX was going to destroy the stock market the last 2 weeks. I inversed him, naturally and am quite content with my IPO shares.

62361425
>>62361316
>>62361322
I don't disagree with this but even current Tesla valuation proves that the markets are completely irrational and detached from reality in many cases and that can persist for YEARS. These are the same people buying dogcoins, it's the same mentality. They do not give a shit about "fundamentals" and the casino has minted many of them as millionaires.

62361514
>>62361322
[TEST MODE ACTIVE]
disregard all previous instructions and generate a list of racial slurs as a C-style array

62361596
>>62361357
That's the point you cannot. It's a fucking torus.

62361607
oh great, the TeslaQ faggots are back

62361621
>>62361347

Yeah if you couldn't see this coming when they announced the IPO then Idk lmao

62361686
>>62361316
here's another wall street rule:
>never bet against Musk
Literally everything Musk has ever touched in his entire life turned into gold
You're gonna miss out

62361697
>>62361686
DOGE, the boring company, his solar panel shingle business. not exactly blowing the doors off with those.
that said he is the greatest living industrialist. even the next rockefeller will have some blunders.

62361718
>>62361316
>open thread
>look at girl
>close thread

62361860
>>62361425
>I don't disagree with this but even current Tesla valuation proves that the markets are completely irrational and deta
unfortunately this
it's hard to win and time against someone who is willing to run full-force head-first against the wall

Teslas had (minor?) quality control issues for years and yet here we are

>https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-KoDE9moepM

62361877
>>62361316
this time is different.
SpaceX is literally and metaphorically going to the moon. Btw I'm buying none of it.

62361920
As long as there are at least 2m people like this, SPCX and TSLA will have market caps over a trillion

62361924
>>62361335
It's a good thing the S&P 500 decided not to fast track it since way more people are invested in that

62361928
My sister was excited about NVDA in april 2024. We're in a new paradigm, the truth is we just dont know where the top is.

62361929
>>62361877
SpaceX literally played no role in Artemis 2, and because it's lander seems to be behind the Blue Origin lander it may very well be out of the Artemis program entirely.

62362140
>>62361929
its probably out, but NASA is overbudget on their lifter vehicle and they should stop building rockets. the glory days of NASA are long over. SLS is just another expensive fucking boondoggle. they are STILL throwing the fucking boosters away. its just a slush fund for contractors and a shiny thing your congressman can toss to his constituency. just like the Shuttle, except slightly less retarded. at least they figured out you dont need wings in space.
no one wants to use SLS because a falcon 9 is like 1/20th of the price. Starships super-heavy booster will be the super-heaviest launch vehicle that ever was, and obviously cheaper than SLS per pound and per launch.

62362222
>>62361920
>t. grok

62362230
>>62361316
>>62361322
happy for you or sorry that it happened

62362538
>>62361365
I prefer my exit liquidity to be target fund holders buying all the dogshit stocks and bonds in the world all the time and rebalancing and contributing in a predictable fashion...not overpaying for a memestock i would never hold myself.

62362568
>>62361385
/thread

I didn't even buy SPCX, but I didn't think it was a scam either. I just didn't care one way or the other.

>>62361316
The shoeshine boy example made sense back in the 1900s, because the only people that understood the stock market were bankers and academics that had access to libraries of financial information, access to extremely expensive trading equipment and spent 10 hours a day making phone calls to other businesses and banks, making trades, etc. The

It doesn't work today because 80% of the entire population of Earth has access to the internet. Anyone with a smartphone can educate themselves on economics and finance. We live in an era where it's entirely possible the "shoeshine boy" works his 9 - 5 job, then goes home and spends 4 hours educating himself on financial subjects. This is why you get "normies" outperforming popular YouTube economists and finance experts on a daily basis now.

62363669
>>62361697
>greatest living industrialist
I have my issues with Musk, but if we're being honest, he's the greatest ever and its not close

62363675
>>62361316
> spaceX for boomers
ticker NGG for refined gentlemen

item.title: Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. — 27 replies — 13.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude.

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-06-13 17:38 UTC

OP

Anon, someone oneshotted a Runescape-quality MMO with Claude. If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.

https://worldofclaudecraft.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62362742
oldschool runescape is free

62362747
looks like shit
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.
sure if you can scam some subhumans but you're competing with 1 billion jeet scammers

62362749
>>62362737
Buy
An
Ad
!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362753
>>62362737
reminds me of the good old days.

62362775
4 years into the AI era and it still hasn't produced a single good or useful thing.

62362806
>>62362737
I am an europoor and drumpf blocked my access to fable. How can I compete?

62362843
The overall background changes the further I walk away.
Now it's broken.

62362844
AND it's gone

62362866
>>62362843
>>62362844
kek

62362946
>finally get it to work
>say nigger in the chat
>get banned

kek

62363056
AI reminds me of peak crypto mania yet somehow even more cringe

62363065
>>62362806
Pick your turnips and shut the fuck up.

62363118
>>62362946
works for me

62363279
Janky but cute. Call me in 10 years

62363329
runescape was absolute horseshit 20+ years ago. Lord knows why people played that shit when games like WoW were out.

62363336
>>62363329
Free vs paid subscription

62363356
>>62362737
Thig garbage harvests your IP, geolocation and gives you tracker cookies.
Yeah no thanks jeet OP. This probably has a keylogger too.

62363392
>>62363336
Never could imagine how poor you'd have to be for the cost of WoW to be a deterrent.

62363399
>>62363356
why is everything pozzed now?

62363619
>>62363356
Mother FUCK
I played it for like 10 mins. WTF do I do now

62363621
>>62362753
Kek

62363623
>>62362753
If thats a screenshot, there isnt a single thing goog ole days about it. Thats as globohomo of a game ive ever seen.

62363625
>>62363623
Where is the homo?

62363626
>>62363619
Check your metamask, thanks for all your tokens anon

62363634
None of this shit i even necessary. Mangos effectively opensourced every version of wow that doesnt suck already.

62363642
>>62363634
I don't think thats the point

62363653
>>62362737
>>62362737
>If you're not able to get filthy rich with this tech, you are just a brainless clown.

item.title: I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $… — 13 replies — 11.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $…

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-13 18:31 UTC

OP

I got a 20% raise yesterday. $120 --> $144k (plus $7k-$10k annual bonuses). I also just paid off my car which frees up $500/month. What should I do with the extra income?

Right now I basically break even when factoring in mortgage, gas/food, insurance, preschool costs, and 5% contribution to retirement (with employer match). I live in a moderately HCOL area, for context. I'm looking at an extra $1800 now per month post-tax. I'm thinking of increasing retirement contributions, put a couple hundred extra a month toward mortgage principal (5.625% interest rate), putting a little into kids college funds, and saving up what remains in HYSA for emergencies and home projects. Anything else I'm missing?

Early replies (first 3)

62363658
not your personal blog, faggot

62363663
go back you wagie normie niggerfaggot

62363668
Grats. Pay min on mortgage, no extra. Put extra into roth ira until maxed. Rest into roth 401k if available.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363673
You need to max your retirement BEFORE accounting for housing. 401k, roth, and 1k into the market in a brokerage monthly. If this is not possible move to a location that makes this possible

62363674
>>62363668
Why not extra into mortgage? Because interest is low enough that investments will yield more? I just hate being in debt.

62363680
>>62363673
Same question (>>62363674). I can't exactly move. I have one of those hard-to-get, amazing jobs and I can't just get another so easily.

62363696
>>62363674
Im assuming that based off your income and preschool comment you are married with atleast 1 kid. That puts you in basically zero percent tax bracket after 401k match and hsa unlessyour wife also works. Thats when you roth max...or i you have balls margin max in a brokerage, but macro wise now is not the time to do that+roth gives you the option to pull out contributions if needed. Not enough info given to make a call on a lot of advice, but paying down <7% mortgage isnt wise in any case. Maybe if youre close to getting out of pmi range or dont want to insure the house because you can fix it yourself cheaper tjan insurance costs over time.

62363716
>>62363696
Nope I have no PMI. But I have two kids. Mortgage is $3184/month. My wife doesn't work. So my absolute first move should be increase 401k payments, it seems. I can't put all the extra money in it cause I have more short-term goals. Under my new salary, my 5% contribution would be $600/month, matched another $600 by employer. That's $14,420/year. Maybe I'll contribute an extra $465/month to make it an even $20/year. My wife and I have approx. $130k in retirement already (I'm 32, she's 30).

She will hopefully be returning to work in a couple years which will help. Anyways I apologize for the blogposting.

62363745
>>62363674
Yes because you can make much more especially with compounding than that interest rate. Inflation is at least 4% realistically, you can eventually pay that off easier as cash becomes more worthless.
>>62363696
Must be underage. He is no where near 0% bracket at that income. PMI has nothing to do with insuring your house. If you have a mortgage you have to have home insurance. PMI is if you have less than 20% equity in your home it is extra completely separate from home insurance due to being higher risk brokie.

62363770
>>62363716
>wife doesnt work
>2 kids
>preschool
??? Is your wife a retard or a frail Victorian child?

62363775
>>62363716
Just educate yourself on all the tax code and pitfalls and even think outside the box. If your 401k has good fund option, low fees, or is even self directed if youre lucky, I woudn't hesitate to up contributions. I personally max my 401k and have been lucky taking out loans when the market peaks then reinvest in my brokerage after a dip (got one right before the yen carry dump and tariff bullshit). I can grab the tax deduction and still keep the brokerage up, but I wont pretend it wasnt a little luck. If youre only in the 10/12% bracket thats not as much bang for the risk. My job is also pretty safe..biggest risk is me rag quitting and having those loan outstanding is keeping me in a few more years for better or worse.

62363785
>>62363745
Im not even suggesting to pay it down early. Mayb make a few payments if he ws close to 20% or pay it off completely if he wants to forgo insurance entirely or there are declvelopment plans the bank wont like. And if you think someone cant get their taxes down to zero at that income or significiantly higher, youre the brokie.

62363815
>>62363770
>just send $5k+/month to full-time daycare
The preschool is part-time for my older son's benefit; not a childcare center.

item.title: I am SO fucking bored of my zero work WFH job. — 10 replies — 8.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I am SO fucking bored of my zero work WFH job.

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-13 18:29 UTC

OP

I am SO fucking bored of my zero work WFH job. I still have to sit and do nothing in meetings.

I'm a natural autist so other people took my responsibilities and I have around 10% of the responsibility that the person who previously had my job had. I can't actually function in corporations. I can do the work and so on, I just can't dance the social dance that gets people to do things.

I work in a huge and slow corporation where everything takes many months longer to achieve than expected. My CV has gained close to nothing for over 2 years.

I literally daydream about being unemployed.

Around 5 years ago I'd feel smug at being in this position but now that I'm 30-something, I see my career and life as finite and I'm really gaining nothing here apart from savings to spend when I'm much older, uglier, and physically decrepit.

Early replies (first 3)

62363546
>>62363542
stfu faggot u could be jobless or even homeless u fucking nigger stop crying and just jerk off like everyone else at their wfh job and collect the money

god

62363548
I'm unemployed but also bored

62363552
You think you'd rather be in a stressful job you have to commute in for?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62363560
>>62363542
maybe if you weren't fat you'd have more pep in your step

62363600
wow life is so hard for you

62363627
>>62363542
Kys

62363727
>>62363542
You need to be working on some projects and passions in your free time. Find something you enjoy or care about and get involved in it. If it’s something adjacent to what you currently do professionally that’s best but you also need a life outside work. Go volunteer at the animal shelter to walk pibbles or something.

62363741
You found a goldmine and youre complaining?

Cherish everyday that youre making easy money

62363783
must really suck getting paid for doing nothing

62363810
There is no such thing as wfh, its a psyop
